commit.message:
fix(audit): avoid websocket timeout double-counting

Summary:
- stop websocket chat runs from logging both timed_out and succeeded for the same request
- add an end-to-end regression test that asserts timeout runs only emit the timed_out lifecycle event

Rationale:
- keep runtime observability counts accurate for websocket failures and alerting
- cover the review comment with a concrete audit regression check

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/ws.py backend/tests/test_e2e_conversation.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_e2e_conversation.py tests/test_chat_api.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
docs(plan): replace nested roadmap with workstreams

Summary:
- replace the mixed sections/seasons/batches navigation with one live planning model built around workstreams
- turn the overview roadmap into a single master plan file and add one checklist-based file per workstream
- demote the old next-steps and status-report pages to legacy pointers so the live docs stop competing with each other

Rationale:
- make planning status readable at a glance with one master entry point and one consistent split of work
- remove the misleading multi-layer roadmap structure from the live docs

Tests:
- cd docs && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
InteractiveUtils: In `edit(m::Module)`, throw a more informative error when `pathof(m)` is nothing (#61303)

🤖 Discovered via automated Codex audit.

Before this PR, `edit(m::Module)` will throw a relatively unhelpful
error if `pathof(m)` is nothing:

```julia
julia> module MyModule end

julia> InteractiveUtils.edit(MyModule)
ERROR: object is not callable
Stacktrace:
```

After this PR, it throws a more useful error.

🤖 Co-authored-by: Codex

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): pin npm registry to public npmjs

Summary:
- add a repo-level npm registry config for the public npmjs registry
- regenerate the lockfile so resolved package URLs no longer point at a
  private Nexus host
- add a repository guard that prevents private registry settings from leaking
  back into tracked npm inputs

Rationale:
- make GitHub Actions installs deterministic and independent of local user npm
  configuration
- prevent future CI failures caused by lockfiles generated against private
  workstation registries

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/tooling.test.ts
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(review): sync blocking GitHub review feedback to Linear

Summary:
- add a github-review workflow that runs after successful make-all PR runs
- mirror blocking GitHub review findings into Linear and move issues back to
  Rework through a dedicated sync script
- require Linear issue identifiers in PR bodies and include recent Linear
  comments in the agent prompt for rework runs

Rationale:
- make external GitHub review part of the normal PR flow without giving GitHub
  authority over work state
- keep Linear as the single source of truth for rework dispatch while still
  surfacing reviewer feedback back to Symphony
- enforce a stable PR-to-Linear mapping so review results can be synced
  deterministically

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

[feat](trx-rs): add FT2 decoder protocol support

Implement a distinct FT2 protocol path in the decoder stack and align\nits timing with the confirmed FT2 framing used by Decodium.\n\nCo-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Signed-off-by: Stanislaw Grams <stanislawgrams@gmail.com>

commit.message:
chore(repo): align workflow and ci conventions

Summary:
- pin CI and local defaults to Node 24 LTS and update GitHub Actions
- require branch and PR handoff details in the shipped workflow prompt
- remove stale Elixir-specific repo tooling references and cache cleanup

Rationale:
- stabilize GitHub Actions by avoiding the flaky Node 25 path and old action
  runtimes
- make the agent handoff contract match the intended branch and PR flow
- keep repo docs, skills, and workspace cleanup aligned with the current
  TypeScript implementation

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

[fix](trx-frontend-http): group decode history by decoder

Serve grouped decode history payloads and restore each decoder through
explicit history restore hooks instead of replaying a mixed message stream.

This reduces replay overhead further by removing type regrouping and keeping
history restoration on decoder-specific bulk paths.

commit.message:
Add a compact workout split builder below the calculator (#22)

* feat(ui): add a compact workout split builder

Summary:
- add split builder state and UI below the calculator with add,
  save, select, copy, and delete actions
- capture the current calculator values into compact split rows that
  show distance, pace, and time together
- tighten headings, spacing, and mobile layout to fit the new split
  workflow without horizontal overflow
- cover the new DOM flow with add/select/save/duplicate/delete tests

Rationale:
- lets runners turn the live calculator into a readable workout plan
  instead of re-entering each segment elsewhere
- preserves the current calculator editing model by saving stable
  state snapshots and only switching to save mode after real edits
- keeps the new planning UI compact enough for common mobile and
  laptop viewports

Tests:
- npm run check
- built-preview visual verification on desktop and mobile via a
  temporary Puppeteer run against dist

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs: refresh split-builder docs and PR draft

Summary:
- add the workout split builder to the shipped-feature list in README
- replace the stale PR draft and title metadata with HEL-30 details
- record the desktop and mobile preview notes used for this UI pass

Rationale:
- keeps the branch documentation aligned with the published behavior
- lets the repo PR scripts create accurate metadata for the current issue
- preserves the visual verification notes alongside the branch history

Tests:
- npm test -- tests/create-pr.test.js tests/handoff.test.js

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(ui): flatten split rows into inline editor lines

Summary:
- turn workout split items from stacked cards into dense single-line
  rows with inline actions
- keep the existing select/save behavior while shrinking the split row
  height and preserving visible distance, pace, and time values
- add a static markup/CSS guard for the split builder row layout and
  refresh the PR preview notes for the clarified acceptance criteria

Rationale:
- matches the later CEO clarification that the split list should feel
  like a compact workout editor rather than a card stack
- keeps the mobile experience dense while still exposing all three
  runner-facing metrics and the inline row actions
- makes the visual evidence and PR metadata reflect the shipped UI

Tests:
- npm test -- tests/main.test.js tests/accessibility.test.js
- built-preview desktop/mobile verification via a temporary Puppeteer
  run against dist

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
[fix](trx-frontend-http): move history decode off main thread

Serve a dedicated decode-history worker and move compressed history fetch
and CBOR parsing into that worker.

The main thread now drains ready-made decode batches within a frame budget,
which further reduces UI disruption during large history restores.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
[fix](trx-frontend-http): batch decode history replay

Replay decode history in decoder-specific batches instead of feeding every
message through the single-message path.

This reduces per-message array churn and UI scheduling during large history
loads while keeping the existing live decode behavior unchanged.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
Signed-off-by: Stan Grams <sjg@haxx.space>

commit.message:
[fix](trx-frontend-http): speed up decode history replay

Serve decode history as gzipped CBOR and decode it in the frontend.
Defer map materialization until replay completes to avoid replay-time stutter,
and include the pending longest-QSO style adjustment.

commit.message:
test(coverage): targeted unit tests for decision_api and saas_client (Issue #30) (#31)

Add 29 new scenarios across decision_api and saas_client to raise coverage:

decision_api.lua:
- Jitter hash fallbacks (crc32_short, hmac_sha256, sha1_bin unavailable)
- Provider detection (anthropic, openai_compatible, gemini, mistral, nil)
- ip_tor normalization via normalize_boolish (yes/no/1/0)
- Bundle descriptor hint suppresses user_agent when needs_user_agent=false
- access_handler 503 paths (rule engine missing, no bundle loaded)
- LLM rejection JSON body for tpm_exceeded and tpd_exceeded
- Retry-after gt_3600 bucket metric
- log_handler upstream error queues saas event, skips 429
- debug_session_handler: 404 (no secret), 405 (GET), 403 (wrong secret), 204 (valid)
- debug_logout_handler: 404 (no secret), 405 (GET), 204 (POST)

saas_client.lua:
- flush_events/pull_config guard when not initialized
- Edge token CR/LF injection uses empty bearer auth
- Event coalescing: same signature increments counter, summary on flush
- Subject ID hashing removes raw field, sets subject_id_hash
- Non-retriable 401 on event flush counts as error
- Bundle load rejection ack as rejected
- Half-open circuit failure reopens circuit
- Heartbeat JSON string body extract_payload branch
- Timer premature=true callback returns without HTTP request

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[feat](trx-client): add configurable decode history retention

Add a default decode history retention setting in minutes with per-rig overrides, resolve the active rig retention in the HTTP frontend runtime, and apply that retention consistently across backend decode history buffers and frontend decoder views. This removes fixed APRS, HF APRS, AIS, VDES, FT8, and WSPR browser-side history caps in favor of time-based pruning, and includes the pending longest-QSO card style reset.

commit.message:
test(coverage): targeted unit tests for Issue #25 coverage gate (#28)

* test(coverage): add targeted unit tests for issue #25 coverage gate

Add new test cases for previously uncovered branches in utils.lua,
streaming.lua, and bundle_loader.lua to push non-generated coverage
above the 82.28% baseline:

- utils: now() no-ngx, safe_require, to_hex, constant_time_equals,
  encode_base64 no-ngx, decode_base64 fallback, base64url_decode edge
  cases, sha256 nil guard, json additional branches, get_json chain
- streaming: validate_config type errors, is_streaming nil/non-table,
  body_filter truncated/buffer paths, include_partial_usage=false
- bundle_loader: ua descriptor hint, sha1-fallback HMAC, hmac
  unavailable, md5/pure hash fallbacks, no-timer init, file_not_found,
  saas_client audit event on apply

Closes #25

commit.message:
feat(capture): render scaffolds without writing files

Summary:
- add capture rendering APIs that return generated driver and simulator
  source directly instead of always writing files
- support module-name strings, signal-name overrides, and formatted
  generated source for capture-backed scaffolds
- add focused tests for the render APIs and include ex_dna as a
  dev/test dependency

Rationale:
- make capture generation easier to reuse from tooling and tests
  without forcing filesystem writes as an intermediate step
- keep generated scaffold output more controllable and predictable
  for both direct generation and future editor or LLM-assisted flows

Validation:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- mix test test/ethercat/capture_test.exs --warnings-as-errors

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(evals): add runtime reliability harness

Summary:
- add a deterministic runtime eval harness that exercises fallback-capable chat, onboarding, strategist, daily briefing, and shell timeout flows
- cover the new harness with dedicated tests and document how to run it from the backend testing guide
- update the Season 1 runtime reliability roadmap docs to reflect the shipped eval harness and the remaining open work

Rationale:
- give S1-B3 a repeatable reliability check that does not depend on live LLM providers
- keep fallback wiring and degraded behavior easy to verify after future runtime changes

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/evals/__init__.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_eval_harness.py tests/test_daily_briefing.py tests/test_agent.py tests/test_strategist.py tests/test_shell_tool.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run python -m src.evals.harness --scenario daily_briefing_fallback --indent 2

- distinguish aggregate links from concrete endpoints in frame and
  link telemetry

  log noise

- bounded reason and fault kinds are easier to consume in dashboards,

commit.message:
feat(vault): add session-scoped secret references

Summary:
- add opaque session-scoped secret references and resolve them at tool execution time
- expose a new `get_secret_ref` path so skills can use stored credentials without pulling raw values back into model context
- update the Season 1 trust-boundary docs and Moltbook skill guidance to reflect the safer secret flow

Rationale:
- raw `get_secret()` retrieval kept a trust-boundary gap open because the secret value had to pass back through the agent loop
- session-bound refs narrow that gap while keeping authenticated downstream tool usage practical

Tests:

commit.message:
fix(issue-4): use LLM-aware estimator for circuit breaker cost resolution (with E2E) (#23)

* fix(issue-4): use LLM-aware estimator for circuit breaker cost resolution

* test(e2e): add regression test for LLM cost resolution

* test(issue-4): add UC-09/UC-16/UC-17 integration coverage and fix E2E test

- Remove fake test_header_hint.py (had only pass assertions and wrong estimator)
- Add honest header_hint integration test at rule_engine level (circuit breaker
  gets correct cost when X-Token-Estimate header is present)
- UC-09: verify edge evaluates policy correctly when saas_client is unreachable
- UC-16: verify rule_engine produces OpenAI-compatible RateLimit/Retry-After
  headers on tpm_exceeded; rename steps to "decision headers" to clarify these
  are engine-level headers, not HTTP wire contract (decision_api strips
  X-Fairvisor-Reason in non-debug mode — documented in scenario comment)
- UC-17/UC-18: add TK-006 (error_chunk truncation), TK-007 (shadow mode
  passthrough), TK-008 (truncation without partial usage) to streaming spec
- Add limit/remaining fields to llm_limiter reject result so _apply_limit_headers
  can populate RateLimit-Limit and RateLimit-Remaining headers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): make UC-09 actually exercise saas_client failure path

Previously the UC-09 scenario used an allow decision, which exits before
calling queue_event — so saas_unreachable flag had no effect on the test.

Now uses a reject decision (TPM exceeded) which does call queue_event.
Track saas_event_attempts in mock to assert the call was made.
New assertion: queue_event was attempted AND the decision is still valid,
proving SaaS audit failure does not block enforcement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(e2e): P0 e2e tests for Issue #4 — OpenAI contract and streaming truncation

- decision_api.lua: wire llm_limiter.build_error_response in reject path;
  tpm_exceeded/tpd_exceeded/prompt_tokens_exceeded/max_tokens_per_request_exceeded
  now return Content-Type: application/json with OpenAI error structure before ngx.exit(429)
- tests/e2e/test_llm_openai_contract.py: 5 tests verifying 429 body is valid OpenAI
  JSON (error.type=rate_limit_error, error.code=rate_limit_exceeded, message references reason)
  plus Content-Type and RateLimit-* headers still present
- tests/e2e/test_llm_streaming.py: 5 tests verifying mid-stream truncation at
  max_completion_tokens=10 — events 1+2 pass, event 3 triggers truncation,
  response ends with finish_reason=length and [DONE]
- tests/e2e/policy_llm_openai_contract.json: TPM=500, header_hint estimator
- tests/e2e/policy_llm_streaming.json: TPM=100k, defaults.max_completion_tokens=10,
  streaming.buffer_tokens=1, graceful_close mode
- tests/e2e/sse_fixture.txt: 3 SSE events (4+5+6 tokens) + [DONE]
- tests/e2e/mock-streaming-backend.conf: nginx serving sse_fixture.txt as SSE
- docker-compose.test.yml: edge_llm_openai_contract (18089), mock_streaming_backend,
  edge_llm_streaming (18090)
- conftest.py: fixtures for edge_llm_openai_contract_base_url and edge_llm_streaming_base_url

All 497 unit+integration tests continue to pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): resolve code-quality warnings and add new e2e tests to smoke CI

- test_llm_openai_contract.py: remove unused 'import json'
- test_llm_streaming.py: remove unused 'import pytest'
- conftest.py: invert fixture conditionals (if not ready: skip / return url)
  to eliminate mixed implicit/explicit return warnings from static analysis
- bin/ci/run_e2e_smoke.sh: add test_llm_openai_contract.py and
  test_llm_streaming.py to smoke test suite so new E2E tests are
  gated in CI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(e2e): replace nginx streaming mock with Python server to fix 405 on POST

nginx's static file module only handles GET/HEAD — serving sse_fixture.txt
via try_files returned 405 for POST /v1/chat/completions, causing all 5
streaming e2e tests to fail.

Replace mock_streaming_backend with a minimal Python http.server that:
- Accepts POST on any path → returns SSE fixture with correct Content-Type
- Accepts GET / → returns "ok" (used by healthcheck)
- Embeds the SSE content inline (4+5+6 token events for truncation test)

Remove broken mock-streaming-backend.conf and sse_fixture.txt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(streaming): preserve buffered events when truncation fires mid-batch

When nginx buffers multiple SSE events into a single body_filter call,
the early return on truncation was discarding events that had already
passed the token budget check and been added to `out`.

Fix: return table_concat(out) .. _build_termination(ctx) so that events
processed before the truncation threshold are forwarded to the client.

For single-event calls (the common streaming case), out is empty so
table_concat({}) = "" — identical behavior to before.

This was caught by the e2e streaming truncation test where the Python
mock sends the complete SSE fixture as a single HTTP response body.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[fix](trx-frontend-http): smooth decode history loading

Reduce main-thread stalls while decode history replays.

Coalesce decoder list redraws and map maintenance so spectrum
and controls stay responsive during history import.

Verification: node --check on modified frontend JS files.

commit.message:
feat(api): add swagger ui and tasks endpoint (#9)

Summary:
- add /tasks API with Kinexo lookup and response schema
- serve /openapi.json and local Swagger UI assets with env toggle
- document swagger usage and env defaults

Rationale:
- provide interactive API docs and try-it-out flow
- expose OpenAPI details and security scheme for testing

Tests:
- npm install --cache /tmp/npm-cache
- npm start + curl http://localhost:9090/docs
- curl http://localhost:9090/openapi.json
- curl http://localhost:9090/tasks (expected 400 without q)
- ENV=production -> /docs and /openapi.json return 404

Co-authored-by: Wylliam <w.delaunay@groupeonepoint.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: add advanced browser actions (#26)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(api): add tasks endpoint and swagger docs (#10)

Summary:
- add /tasks handler composing client search and active tasks
- mark upstream failures and return structured 502/500 errors
- serve OpenAPI spec plus /docs Swagger UI

Rationale:
- provide the US-005 single-call task lookup experience
- document the endpoint with an explicit response schema

Tests:
- node -e 'const {createServer}=require("./src"); const server=createServer(); server.listen(0, async ()=>{ const port=server.address().port; const res1=await fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/tasks`); console.log("/tasks", res1.status, await res1.text()); const res2=await fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/openapi.json`); const json=await res2.json(); console.log("/openapi.json", res2.status, json.paths && Object.keys(json.paths).length); const res3=await fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/docs`); console.log("/docs", res3.status); server.close(); });'

Co-authored-by: Wylliam <w.delaunay@groupeonepoint.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
[fix](trx-frontend-http): correct map auth and overlay behavior

Treat the SPA entry routes as public so direct requests to /map,
/decoders, /settings, and /about return the app shell and let
the frontend show the login screen instead of a 403.

Move the map filter overlay to the bottom-right corner and color
decode contact paths by their decoded band so they match the band
legend and locator overlays.

Verified with cargo test -p trx-frontend-http.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Signed-off-by: Stan Grams <sjg@haxx.space>

[feat](trx-reporting): add software comment to APRS beacon

Append the APRS-IS position beacon comment as
`trx-rs v<version> by SP2SJG` so IGate beacons identify
the running software version.

Update the APRS beacon formatter tests to assert the exact
payload including the generated version string.

commit.message:
feat: add diff-aware QA and portable browser sessions (#22)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(linear): add debug request logging

Summary:
- expose the current Linear issue id in the workflow prompt so Codex can
  use linear_add_issue_comment without looking up the active issue first
- add SYMPHONY_LOG_LEVEL support and debug-only Linear request logging for
  both the tracker client and the Codex linear_graphql tool path
- document the debug logging workflow and cover the new behavior with
  repository, workflow, tracker, logger, and app-server tests

Rationale:
- reduce Codex guesswork around current issue lookup and completion
  comments
- make failing and successful Linear requests inspectable on demand
  without increasing default log noise

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

merge: thruster 패치 업데이트 통합

- 기능 변경 없는 의존성 갱신 흐름 통합

- 기능 개발 커밋과 운영 의존성 갱신 커밋의 구분 관리

- 스냅샷의 완료 이력 및 Codex 상호작용 정보 확장

Rationale:

- 대시보드와 API의 장애 분석 활용도 향상을 위한 런타임 정보 강화

fix(context): fall back when tiktoken is unavailable

Summary:
- add approximate token counting when the cl100k encoding cannot be loaded
- cover the offline fallback path in context window tests
- update runtime reliability roadmap docs to reflect the shipped degraded-mode hardening work

Rationale:
- keep the context window path working when the runtime is offline or tokenizer assets cannot be fetched
- move Season 1 reliability work forward while keeping docs aligned with actual progress

- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_context_window.py

Rationale:

- 패치 업데이트의 별도 커밋 분리를 통한 추적 및 롤백 판단 용이성 확보

chore: type ship branch workflow (#18)

Closes #17

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(spec): document ssh worker extension updates

Summary:
- SPEC의 SSH 워커 확장 및 상태 규칙 변경 내용 반영
- 후속 구현 작업 범위 고정을 위한 계획 문서 추가

Rationale:

- 워커 실행 환경 확장에 따른 스케줄링 제약과 설정 의미 명확화 필요

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(approvals): queue and mark auto-resume replays

Summary:
- add a dedicated websocket resume message type so auto-resume replays do not persist duplicate user turns
- queue pending approval resumes until the websocket reconnects and can replay them safely
- add an e2e regression test for non-duplicating resume behavior

Rationale:
- address PR feedback that approvals could silently stall during reconnects
- keep the auto-resume path from duplicating user messages in stored session history

Tests:
- cd backend && python3 -m py_compile src/models/schemas.py src/api/ws.py tests/test_e2e_conversation.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_approvals_api.py tests/test_chat_api.py tests/test_e2e_conversation.py tests/test_websocket.py
- cd frontend && npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
